business.headline: Why do only 20% of women reach senior roles in the same company vs 49% men?Many organisations hire women in good numbers, yet far fewer make it to leadership positions within the same company. The gap raises an important question: what happens along the way that slows down women's career progression?

Adani Total Gas hikes industrial gas prices amid Middle East tensionsThe supply challenges come after attacks on Iran and retaliatory strikes by Tehran, which have disrupted shipping through the Strait of Hormuz. The narrow waterway between Iran and Oman is one of the world's most important energy routes.

Still think rural women aren't financially savvy? See how they manage moneyThe common belief is that rural women are not financially aware. Yet daily life in many households tells a different story. Women are budgeting carefully, building small savings and guiding family spending. Could rural women actually be the silent managers of household finances?

Tax deadlines coming up? Know the last dates for revised returns and advance taxTax deadlines tend to creep up quietly, and many taxpayers realise the dates only when time is running out. If you still need to correct your income tax return or plan to pay advance tax, it helps to know the key deadlines and what options are available.

India back on Russia's oil map as Iran war tightens supplyTwo oil tankers that were earlier expected to head towards East Asia have now switched their destination to India. Data from Kpler and Vortexa show the ships are carrying a combined 1.4 million barrels of Urals crude, a Russian oil grade.

Reliance shares gain 3%: Why is RIL rising today amid Iran warBrokerages said disruptions in the Middle East could tighten the supply of refined oil products globally. This could push refining margins higher, which directly benefits companies like Reliance that process crude oil into fuels such as diesel and petrol.

Rupee stabilises after record low, but oil price swings threaten outlookFor currency markets, the rupee's brief recovery may only be an interlude, as traders brace for more turbulence driven by oil prices and the shifting geopolitics of West Asia.

Sensex, Nifty up: Why is stock market rising today despite crude oil concerns?At 9:30 am, the S&P BSE Sensex was trading higher by 245.39 points at 79,361.58. The NSE Nifty50 was also up 85.50 points at 24,566.00.
Sensex, Nifty open in green as markets snap 3-day losing streak; RIL up 2%The S&P BSE Sensex was up 245.39 points to 79,361.58, while the NSE Nifty50 gained 85.50 points to 24,566.00 as of 9:30 am.

Sensex, Nifty opening: Will stock market open higher today?At 8:30 am, Gift Nifty futures were trading at 24,677.00. This indicates that the benchmark Nifty 50 may open above Wednesday's closing level of 24,480.5.

How companies can embrace AI without losing controlAlmost every major organisation now uses AI in customer-facing products. But this rapid adoption has exposed an uncomfortable truth. Governance frameworks have not kept pace with the speed of innovation.

Sensex falls 1,110 points, Nifty below 24,500; Tata Steel tumbles 7%The BSE Sensex fell 1,122.66 points, or 1.40%, to close at 79,116.19, while the Nifty 50 declined 385.20 points to settle at 24,480.50.

Tata Steel, L&T, JSW Steel: Why these stocks fell up to 8% todayShares of Tata Steel, Larsen & Toubro and JSW Steel plunged sharply as global tensions and rising crude oil prices rattled markets. Read on to understand what triggered the sell-off today.

Strait of Hormuz shutdown: Is India staring at an energy crisis?Iran has announced the closure of the Strait of Hormuz, a key global oil route. Read on to understand what it could mean for India's energy supplies and economy.

Rupee slumps to all-time low against US dollar: Why is it falling?The rupee came under pressure as escalating tensions between the US, Israel and Iran triggered global risk aversion, while rising crude oil prices and a stronger dollar weighed on emerging market currencies.

L&T shares tumble over 7%: Why is the stock down today?L&T shares dropped nearly 7% during the session as investors turned cautious amid escalating tensions involving the United States, Israel and Iran.

Sensex down 1,500 points: Why is the stock market falling today?Dalal Street opened sharply lower on Wednesday as escalating geopolitical tensions and rising crude prices rattled global markets. Here are the key reasons behind today's sharp stock market fall.

Panic grips Dalal Street as war tensions sink Sensex by ovr 1,700 pointsDalal Street investors turned cautious as crude oil prices surged and fears grew that a prolonged war in West Asia could disrupt global energy supplies and stoke inflation.

Is the stock market open or closed on Holi, March 4?Wondering if you can trade stocks on Holi this year? While celebrations continue across India on March 4, here's what you need to know about NSE and BSE trading.

India has enough oil stocks, no immediate plan to hike fuel prices: Govt sourcesIndia has 25 days of crude oil and refined oil stocks and is scouting for alternative sources for importing crude oil, LPG and LNG, government sources have confirmed. There is no immediate plan to raise the price of petrol and diesel, according to government sources. However, there is bound to be impact where gas and oil is concerned with the closure of the Strait of Hormuz. About 80 per cent of the oil that India depends on comes from Iran and through the Strait of Hormuz. The Strait of Hormuz has been shut for the past 48 hours. Energy analyst Chetan Bhutani states that 'strategic reserves can support LPG and LNG demand for about 15 to 20 hot days, if needed, while crude oil reserves are estimated to last about 40 to 45 days in the event of supply disturbances.'

entertainment.headline: Govinda admits he was drawn to doll-like Neelam Kothari, opens up on their love Govinda spoke about his bond with former co-star Neelam Kothari, recalling his admiration for her and the buzz surrounding their chemistry. His latest remarks revisit a memorable era of Bollywood.

Mammootty and Adoor Gopalakrishnan wrap filming on Padayaatra Mammootty's Padayaatra shoot has been wrapped up. Directed by Adoor Gopalakrishnan, the film is expected to hit theatres in the coming months.

Akshay Kumar vs Shikhar Dhawan: Fun cricket match on Bhooth Bangla set. Watch A BTS video of Akshay Kumar and former cricketer Shikhar Dhawan playing cricket during a break on the set of Priyadarshan's upcoming horror-comedy Bhooth Bangla has emerged online.

Pawan Kalyan's Ustaad Bhagat Singh to release on March 19, fills Toxic's slot Pawan Kalyan's Ustaad Bhagat Singh will now release on March 19, taking the slot previously held by Yash's Toxic. The shift also sets up a box office clash with Ranveer Singh's Dhurandhar 2.

Exclusive: Jisoo of BLACKPINK is ready for Bollywood with co-star Seo In-guk Boyfriend on Demand, a new Korean drama, explores AI-powered romantic relationships. The series questions if technology can truly replicate emotional connection amid growing digital dating trends.

Aishwarya-Abhishek Bachchan attend Arjun Tendulkar-Saaniya's wedding. Watch The Bachchan's attended Arjun Tendulkar and Saaniya Chandhok's wedding on Thursday. Abhishek Bachchan and Aishwarya Rai posed for the paps before entering the venue.

Netflix this week: Hello Bachhon, Vladimir, Made in Korea and more Netflix has a diverse slate of releases lined up this week, including titles like Vladimir, Hello Bachhon, War Machine, and Boyfriend On Demand.

Nicola Peltz surprises husband Brooklyn Beckham on his 27th birthday. Watch video Brooklyn Beckham received a heartfelt birthday celebration from his wife Nicola Peltz on his 27th birthday, with his parents David and Victoria Beckham also sharing messages despite ongoing reports of family tension.

Say cheese: Salman Khan poses with Galwan team during action sequence shoot. Pics Salman Khan is currently shooting for his upcoming film, Battle of Galwan. As the shooting is underway in Mumbai, pictures of the superstar with the team have emerged online.

Actor Samantha splashes water on Raj Nidimoru during Holi celebration on film set Actor Samantha Ruth Prabhu celebrated Holi on the sets of Maa Inti Bangaaram by splashing water on Raj Nidimoru and Nandini Reddy. Their film is all set to release in May 2026.

Orry shares cryptic video with Ibrahim Ali Khan, captions it 'say Orry' Orry shared a cryptic video featuring Ibrahim Ali Khan on his birthday with a caption that read, "It's still not too late to say Orry." The post comes weeks after Orry accused Amrita Singh of causing him "emotional trauma."

Rashmika Mandanna and Vijay Deverakonda to resume Ranabaali shoot soon: Report Actors Rashmika Mandanna and Vijay Deverakonda are reportedly preparing to return to the Ranabaali shoot after their wedding. Here's when the couple is expected to resume filming, and why fans are closely watching the schedule.

Vijay Deverakonda jokes about going underground after reception. Watch viral video After a series of vibrant wedding events, actors Vijay Deverakonda and Rashmika Mandanna concluded their celebrations with a grand reception in Hyderabad. Vijay also addressed the paps, noting that they hope to end their festivities.

What makes Dimple Kapadia a joy to work with? Pankaj Kapur explains In an exclusive interview with India Today, Pankaj Kapur shared his thoughts on reuniting with Dimple Kapadia in Jab Khuli Kitaab. The actor also spoke about working with professional actors.

Vijay Deverakonda shields Rashmika at star-studded Hyderabad reception. Watch Vijay Deverakonda shielded Rashmika Mandanna from the crowd at his reception. The video is now going viral on social media.

Jisoo of BLACKPINK, Seo In-guk's chemistry is off the charts | India Exclusive Interview [Eng Subs] In an exclusive chat with India Today's Bhavna, Jisoo and Seo In-guk open up about their Netflix show Boyfriend on Demand, their ideal types, dating signals and even a possible Bollywood project.

Malaika Arora's mother stranded in Dubai amid conflict: Saw missiles intercepted Malaika Arora's mother, Joyce Arora, is stranded in Dubai after flights were suspended amid the ongoing US-Iran conflict. She described the situation as "frightening."

The Kerala Story 2 box office Day 6: Holi lifts weekday business to Rs 20 crore The Kerala Story 2: Goes Beyond crossed Rs 20 crore at the Indian box office after six days, with the Holi holiday boosting weekday collections. The Vipul Shah-produced film saw a slight jump on Tuesday and Wednesday amid mixed reviews.

FX's The Bear to end with Season 5, confirms Jamie Lee Curtis The Bear, an Emmy-winning series from FX and Hulu, will end with its upcoming fifth season. Cast and creators have indicated that the story was always intended to have a finite run, with filming currently underway in Chicago.

general news.headline: Live: Fresh blasts rock Doha; Iranian drones hit Azerbaijan airportUS-Israel-Iran War Live Updates: Further widening the Middle East military conflict, fighting between Israel and the US on one side and Iran on the other intensified over the past several hours. Iran launched multiple waves of missiles toward Israel as the conflict entered its sixth day, while Israel struck Iranian internal security forces, including the Islamic Revolutionary Guard Corps and Basij units. Reports said preparations were underway in Tehran linked to Ali Khamenei's funeral. The US tested a Minuteman III missile from Vandenberg Space Force Base, and the Pentagon named two soldiers killed in Kuwait. A tanker explosion off Kuwait caused an oil spill, while Qatar evacuated residents near the U.S. Embassy. Stay tuned to IndiaToday.in for live updates on the ongoing Middle East conflict.

Tit for tat? Iran hits US tanker hours after torpedo attack on its warshipThe attack on the American tanker comes soon after Iran warned that the US would 'bitterly regret' Wednesday night's strike on the Iranian warship IRIS Dena in the Indian Ocean. The US torpedo attack on the Iranian warship left 87 dead.

Second Iranian warship near Sri Lanka day after US submarine attackIran has requested Sri Lanka to allow the warship enter its waters for safety. The 100-odd crew on board fear they too could be targeted the same way IRIS Dena was sunk on Wednesday by a US torpedo.

Nitish Kumar files Rajya Sabha nomination, speculation over successor in BiharAccompanied by Union Home Minister Amit Shah, Deputy Chief Minister Samrat Chaudhary and other National Democratic Alliance (NDA) candidates, Nitish Kumar filed his nomination at the state Assembly.

Why putting India in dock over US attack on Iran ship is nonsenseHandcrafted ByOur Editors As India celebrated Holi, the US-Israel-Iran conflict took an unholy turn. The war reached India's backyard as a US submarine torpedoed and sank an Iranian warship in the Indian Ocean. The opposition has attacked the Modi government and questioned its silence. Is the blame justified?

Is Indian Ocean India's ocean? Why IRIS Dena sinking has triggered a fightThe US torpedoed an Iranian warship, IRIS Dena, near Sri Lanka in the Indian Ocean, sparking an intense debate over India's role and responsibility in the strategic waters. Does the Indian Ocean mean India's ocean? What about India's maritime jurisdiction and its role as the guardian of the Indian Ocean?

Claude hit Infosys and TCS stocks, now SaaS firm wants to hire an AI agent directlyAt a time when humans are finding themselves replaced by AI tools, a US-based SaaS company called RevenueCat is switching gears and hiring AI directly. The company has announced that it is looking to hire an AI agent and is willing to pay almost Rs 55 lakh salary to it.

Arjun Tendulkar marries Saaniya Chandhok in star-studded Mumbai weddingArjun Tendulkar married Saaniya Chandhok in a star-studded ceremony in South Mumbai on March 5. Several prominent personalities from cricket, Bollywood and business attended the celebrations alongside the Tendulkar family.

Virosh, the first wedding since Virushka that truly broke the Internet. Here's whyThe last celebrity wedding that genuinely shifted the visual conversation was when Virat Kohli married Anushka Sharma in Tuscany in 2017. In 2026, Rashmika Mandanna and Vijay Deverakonda have had a similar impact.

Military conflict not a solution: PM calls for early end to Iran, Ukraine warsPrime Minister Narendra Modi called for an early end to the wars in Ukraine and the Middle East, stressing that dialogue and diplomacy remain the only path to resolving global conflicts.

Nitish Kumar's exit sparks power tussle, Samrat Chaudhary ahead in Bihar CM raceNitish Kumar, who has dominated Bihar's political landscape for more than two decades, filed his nomination for the Rajya Sabha elections on Thursday, paving the way for his exit from the top post just four months after being sworn in for a record 10th term as Chief Minister.

Target was hit: Israel shares video of its F-35 downing Iran jet in historic killThe Israeli Air Force released footage showing an F-35 'Adir' jet shooting down an Iranian Yak-130 over Tehran, marking the first recorded air-to-air kill of a piloted aircraft by an F-35.
I'm a gynaecologist who believes hormones need habits, not pillsIn my years as a gynaecologist, one truth stands clear. Hormonal health is built daily, not prescribed instantly.

How Robotic Knee Replacement is Redefining the Journey Back to MobilityEarly consultation with an orthopaedic specialist is essential. Delaying surgery in severe cases can lead to worsening deformity and reduced functional outcomes.

IRIS Dena, Indian Navy's guest, hit without warning: Iran says US will regret itIran's foreign minister, Seyed Abbas Aragchi, has called the US strike on the Iranian warship, IRIS Dena, an "atrocity at sea". Aragchi noted that the vessel had been visiting the Indian Navy as part of an exercise and carried about 130 sailors. Aragchi warned that Washington would "bitterly regret" setting such a precedent.

What is the 'doomsday' nuclear missile the US just tested?The US Air Force has test-fired the Minuteman III, a nuclear-capable missile, as the US-Israel-Iran conflict rages on. Here is everything you need to know about the 'doomsday' missile and whether it could trigger a nuclear winter.

US court orders customs to refund $130 billion collected from illegal Trump tariffsJudge Richard Eaton of the U.S. Court of International Trade in Manhattan ordered the government to finalise the cost of bringing millions of shipments into the U.S. without assessing a tariff, according to a court filing. He ordered the refunds to be made with interest.

US tests nuclear-capable 'doomsday' missile, Minuteman III, as Iran war ragesOn Tuesday night, the Air Force Global Strike Command launched the unarmed Minuteman III ICBM from Vandenberg Space Force Base in California, equipped with two test re-entry vehicles, the US Space Force said in a statement.

Punjabi-origin influencer, a Khalistani-critic, stabbed to death at Canada homeNancy Grewal, a Punjabi-origin social media influencer in Canada had frequently posted videos criticising Khalistani activities.

Like Kohli, Smith and Williamson before him, he needs that one defining innings to cement his place among cricket's elite.

Pakistan player fined after alleged misconduct with female hotel staff in Sri Lanka T20 World Cup: Pakistan cricket has landed in fresh controversy after a T20 World Cup squad player was reportedly fined for alleged misconduct with a female hotel staff member in Sri Lanka. The player's identity has not been disclosed.

How dew will impact T20 World Cup semifinal in Mumbai As India face England in the T20 World Cup semi-final at Mumbai's Wankhede Stadium, the toss and the evening dew could prove decisive. Amir says things about India to stay in the news: Kaif tears into pacer Former India batter Mohammed Kaif tore into Pakistan pacer Mohammad Amir for making inflammatory comments about the Indian cricket team. Kaif slammed Amir, stating that he said such things to stay relevant in the news.

Suryakumar Yadav vs Adil Rashid: Ashwin picks key battle for IND vs ENG T20 World Cup: R Ashwin believes Suryakumar Yadav's battle against Adil Rashid could shape the India vs England T20 World Cup semi-final. The former spinner feels India hold the edge at Wankhede but must execute well.

The former England captain believes Kuldeep's record against England makes him a strong tactical option.

History meets high stakes in Mumbai as India face confident England in semifinalIND vs ENG semifinal preview: India and England will meet in the third successive T20 World Cup semifinals, this time at the Wankhede Stadium in Mumbai, where history, rivalry and the pressure of a place in the final converge.

Varun Chakravarthy's ultimate test: Is the spin chakravyuh finally cracking?T20 World Cup: For a long time, it seemed like Varun Chakravarthy was India's most lethal weapon in a T20I. However, a poor outing the Super 8 of the T20 World Cup 2026 leaves the spinner searching for confidence and a bit of spark heading into the semi-final.

India series helped New Zealand a lot in T20 World Cup preparations: Finn AllenT20 World Cup, IND vs NZ: Finn Allen credited New Zealand's pre-World Cup T20I series in India for preparing the team for subcontinental conditions after their dominant nine-wicket semi-final win over South Africa in Kolkata.

South Africa coach's hilarious take on 'chokers' tag: You need a sniff to chokeSouth Africa suffered a heavy nine-wicket defeat to New Zealand in the T20 World Cup semi-final at Eden Gardens. The loss ended their unbeaten run and reignited debate over their performance in knockout matches. However, Shukri Conrad had a rather hilarious take on the choker tag.

India are the favourites, but T20 is a fickle game: Harry Brook before semisT20 World Cup 2026, IND vs ENG: Speaking to reporters, England skipper Harry Brook acknowledged that India would start as favourites in the semi-final at the Wankhede Stadium in Mumbai, especially with home support behind them. However, he also said that T20 cricket is an unpredictable format where momentum can shift rapidly within a matter of overs.

Aiden Markram laments poor batting approach after semi-final exit vs New ZealandSouth Africa captain Aiden Markram blamed his team's batting approach after their semi-final loss to New Zealand. Finn Allen's record century sealed New Zealand's spot in the T20 World Cup final.

Finn Allen's 33-ball hundred in numbers: How special was his Eden blitzT20 World Cup, SA vs NZ: Finn Allen broke quite a few records with his century as New Zealand raced to the final after beating South Africa by nine wickets at the Eden Gardens in Kolkata.

Both you fossils: Dinesh Karthik's unmissable banter with Hussain, AtheronFormer India cricketer Dinesh Karthik has responded strongly to criticism from former England captains over India's Champions Trophy 2025 victory. He highlighted Pakistan's failure to capitalise on similar advantages at the T20 World Cup 2026, pointing to a clear skill gap.

Eden haunts South Africa again as Finn Allen storms Kiwis into T20 World Cup finalUnbeaten till the semi-final, South Africa were knocked out of the T20 World Cup by New Zealand. The Aiden Markram side failed with both bat and ball and ended up losing at Eden Gardens by 9 wickets.

Some things never change: South Africa crumble under semifinal pressure once againSA vs NZ, T20 World Cup 2026 semifinals: South Africa's familiar batting frailties resurfaced at the worst possible time as fans on X tore into the Proteas following another crushing collapse in the T20 World Cup 2026 semifinal in Kolkata.

Daniil Medvedev, Andrey Rublev to miss Indian Wells exhibition amid Middle East tensionsGeopolitical tensions in the Middle East have disrupted international tennis, forcing Daniil Medvedev and Andrey Rublev to miss the Eisenhower Cup at Indian Wells due to Gulf airspace closures.

An honours board for sports journalists: Mumbai celebrates cricket's storytellersIn a rare and thoughtful gesture, the Mumbai Cricket Association unveiled a Journalists' Honours Board at Wankhede Stadium on Wednesday, recognising the writers and broadcasters who have spent decades chronicling cricket's triumphs and memories.

SA vs NZ Highlights, semifinal: Finn Allen's 100 powers New Zealand into finalSouth Africa (SA) vs New Zealand (NZ) Highlights, T20 World Cup: Finn Allen scored an unbeaten 100 as New Zealand beat South Africa by 9 wickets at the Eden Gardens in Kolkata and booked their berth in the final.

Samson jumps 25 places in ICC T20I rankings after 97-not-out vs West IndiesIndia opener Sanju Samson has jumped 25 places in the ICC men's T20I batting rankings on Wednesday, 4 March. The boost in the ranking comes right after Sanju's tremendous show against West Indies.

Pakistan drop Babar Azam for Bangladesh ODI tour after poor T20 World CupPakistan Cricket Board has dropped star batter Babar Azam from the upcoming ODI tour of Bangladesh. This move signals a shift towards fresh talent following Babar's recent poor form in international cricket.

Abhishek Sharma's No.1 rank in danger as Pakistan's Farhan gets closer to top spotT20 World Cup: Abhishek Sharma retained his No. 1 position in the ICC T20I batter rankings, but Pakistan's Sahibzada Farhan has closed in on the top spot following an impressive run in the tournament where he scored 383 runs.

education.headline: The role of data analytics in modern advertising educationThis transformation also exercises a significant impact on the realm of 'advertising education.'

NCERT deletes Azad Pak, adds China's aggression reference in Class 12 textbookThe NCERT has made final changes to its Class 12 Political Science textbook syllabus for the upcoming academic year, including a revision in reference to India's border situation with China, Azad Pakistan, Article 370 and more.

NEET row: Bihar cops find 6 post-dated cheques 'issued for question paper brokers'Six post-dated cheques which were suspected to have been issued in favour of mafia demanding over Rs 30 lakh from each candidate seeking alleged leaked question paper ahead of NEET held last month were recovered by Bihar Police.

Forest fires surge tenfold from 2004 to 2017: Causes, impact and preventionAmidst a relentless surge, intense forest fires are now multiplying tenfold, casting a menacing shadow over our flora and fauna. Year by year, their fiery grip tightens, threatening ecosystems far and wide, now extending their reach to impact human lives.

Fresh plea in Supreme Court to recall NEET results, reconduct examsA fresh petition has been filed in the Supreme Court to recall the National Entrance cum Eligibility Test - Under Graduate (NEET-UG) 2024 results and re-conduct fresh examination after discrepancies in the declared results.

NTA issues clarification after NEET result controversy over toppers, high cut-offIn a detailed statement, the National Testing Agency (NTA) said that the increase in cut-off and number of toppers in the NEET-UG reflect the competitive nature of the exam and maintained that the test's integrity has not been compromised.

Exclusive: Arvind Kejriwal claims 'BJP succession war, PM backing Amit Shah'Speaking exclusively to India Today TV, Delhi Chief Minister Arvind Kejriwal claimed that there is a "succession war" within the BJP. He also claimed that the ruling party is "not fighting the Lok Sabha elections as a team".

4 medical students arrested in Bihar for appearing as proxies for NEET aspirantsFour MBBS students who appeared in place of NEET aspirants in the medical entrance exam were detained in Bihar's Purnea.

NEET-UG question paper not leaked, claims baseless: National Testing AgencyThe reports claiming question paper leak in the medical entrance exam NEET-UG are "completely baseless and without any ground", the National Testing Agency (NTA) has clarified.
The generation with answers: Tech-savvy Indian youth tackling global challengesIndia's tech-savvy youth rewriting the global narrative, one solution at a time.

Left sweeps JNU students' polls, Dhananjay is first Dalit presidentThe Left swept the Jawaharlal Nehru University Students' Union (JNUSU) polls, winning all four posts and trouncing the RSS-affiliated ABVP. UP 12th board exam math, biology papers leaked on WhatsApp, complaint filedThe mathematics and biology question papers for the Uttar Pradesh class 12 board exam were allegedly leaked on a WhatsApp group.

UP Police Constable recruitment exam cancelled days after paper leakThe Uttar Pradesh Police Constable recruitment exam held on February 17 and 18 was canceled due to a paper leak. The Chief Minister has announced a re-examination within the next six months.
